title: NOC 92014 – Supervisors, forest products processing | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 92014 are the essential technical leaders of Canada’s forestry manufacturing sector. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals oversee the conversion of raw timber into pulp, paper, and advanced wood products like cross-laminated timber (CLT). In 2026, as Canada leads the world in sustainable mass-timber construction and eco-friendly packaging, these supervisors are at the absolute forefront of the "Green Building" revolution. For immigration, this role is a top-tier candidate for the BC PNP Skills Immigration and the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Level2Major Group92 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsSub-Major Group920 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsMinor Group9201 – Supervisors, processing and manufacturing occupationsNOC Code92014

common_job_titles: Shift Operating Supervisor - Pulp and Paper
duties: Supervise, coordinate, and schedule the activities of workers who operate pulp and paper mills, sawmills, and wood treatment plants.
Establish work methods to meet production schedules and coordinate activities with other departments (Logistics, Maintenance).
Ensure that systems and equipment are operating efficiently and oversee proper maintenance and repairs.
Resolve operational problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and product quality.
Requisition materials and supplies and manage inventory for processing.
Train workers in job duties, safety procedures, and company policies.
Monitor safety conditions and ensure strict compliance with occupational health and safety (OHS) standards.
Prepare detailed production, safety, and personnel reports for senior management.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Work Experience: Several years of experience in the specific processing or manufacturing occupation supervised are required (e.g., as a sawmill operator or pulper).
Education: A college diploma in pulp and paper technology, wood processing, or a related discipline is often required for senior roles.

Certifications:Lumber Grading Certification is required for grading supervisors.Industrial First Aid certification is standard.A competency certificate in natural gas may be required for specific wood-treating roles.
Lumber Grading Certification is required for grading supervisors.
Industrial First Aid certification is standard.
A competency certificate in natural gas may be required for specific wood-treating roles.
Strong leadership, mechanical aptitude, and proficiency in digital production management systems are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, supervisors in specialized mass-timber (CLT) facilities often command a 10-15% premium over the median industrial wage.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the shift toward "Bio-economy" products—using wood fiber for textiles and biofuels—will create a 10% growth in processing supervisor roles. The primary driver is the need for technically skilled leaders to manage the transition from traditional lumber production to high-value engineered wood products.
labour_market_demand: The demand for forest products processing supervisors is Good, specifically in Canada's timber hubs. In 2026, British Columbia, Quebec, and New Brunswick are reporting a steady need for supervisory talent to manage high-tech sawmills and paper converting facilities. With a wave of retirements in traditional pulp and paper mills, there is a significant "Leadership Gap" that Canadian firms are filling through LMIA-supported international recruitment of experienced production foremen.
Sustainability Innovation: Lead operations in a country at the global forefront of sustainable forest management and high-yield wood technology.
Technological Advancement: Manage "Industry 4.0" mills featuring laser-guided cutting systems, AI-driven sorting, and automated drying kilns.
Economic Stability: Forestry remains a multi-billion dollar cornerstone of the Canadian economy, offering high job security in rural and northern communities.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 2 role is highly valued in Express Entry Category-Based Draws and provincial "Skilled Worker" streams.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure targeting 33,000 workers already in Canada, specifically in essential resource sectors.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: A high-conversion route for supervisors in the forestry sector with a valid job offer in BC.
Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Potential targeting under "Trade Occupations" or specific STEM categories for process engineering.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): Ideal for supervisors in the timber-rich regions of New Brunswick and Nova Scotia.
